untranslatable_reason: This signal requires specialized futures rollover optimization logic that cannot be expressed using standard technical indicators. The rule requires: (1) Parkinson volatility calculation (Vpj) across a 15-day rollover acceptance period—a custom volatility measure not in the allowed indicator set; (2) calendar spread volume percentage (Φj) tracking—requires multi-contract volume analysis; (3) dynamic weight parameter optimization (w between 5-20%); (4) constrained allocation optimization (Nj ≤ w × Φj × NT). These are quantitative portfolio execution parameters requiring custom optimization algorithms, not standard price/volume technical signals. The signal is fundamentally an execution strategy (EVENT category) rather than a tradeable entry/exit rule.
translation_notes: SSRN_3000963_2 is a futures rollover execution algorithm designed to minimize weighted average volatility during the rollover acceptance period by optimizing daily contract allocation across 15 days. While hvol_20 exists as an allowed indicator, it does not capture Parkinson volatility across a rolling 15-day window, nor can standard indicators express the constrained optimization objective. This requires a custom execution module in the backtest engine that implements the mathematical formulation: minimize V̄p = Σ(Nj × Vpj) / N subject to Nj ≤ w × Φj × NT.
